  Manage Serial-over-LAN configuration on one channel. Each set field is written; omitted fields are left alone. Bitrate is applied to both volatile and non-volatile settings since users typically want them in sync.

# ipmi_sol (Resource)

Manage Serial-over-LAN configuration on one channel. Each set field is written; omitted fields are left alone. Bitrate is applied to both volatile and non-volatile settings since users typically want them in sync.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `channel` (Number) IPMI channel (typically 1).

### Optional

- `bitrate` (String) Serial baud rate. One of `9600`, `19200`, `38400`, `57600`, `115200`.
- `cipher_suite` (Number)
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether SOL payloads are accepted on this channel.
- `force_authentication` (Boolean) Require authentication for SOL payloads. Default true.
- `force_encryption` (Boolean) Require encrypted SOL payloads. Default true.
- `host` (String)
- `interface` (String)
- `password` (String, Sensitive)
- `port` (Number)
- `privilege_limit` (String) Min privilege required to open a SOL session: `callback`, `user`, `operator`, `administrator`, `oem`.
- `username` (String)

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `last_updated` (String)
